subject: Re: WARP extreme slowing
body:
Hi Chris and all,

Do you happen to know the solution to the following error when running
Noise2Map?

Unhandled Exception: System.Runtime.InteropServices.SEHException: External
component has thrown an exception at Warp.GPU.Rotate3DExtractAt(UInt64
t_volume, int3 dimsvolume, IntPte d_proj, int3 dimsproj, Single[]
h_positions, UInt32 batch) at Noise2Map.Program.Main(String[] args)

GPU: Quadro RTX 6000

Command: noise2map --observation1 even --observation2 odd --angpix 3.4
--batchsize 4 --dont_flatten_spectrum true --iterations 10000
--dont_augment true --gpuid_preprocess 0 --gpuid_network 0

Thank you so much for your help in advance!

Cheers,

Cyan Ching

PhD student

The Physical Chemistry Curie Lab

On Wed, 10 Aug 2022 at 15:20, Chris Dienemann <chris_dienemann@gmx.de>
wrote:

> Hi emma,
>
> did you check that all the WarpWorkers are still running (the little
> terminals that open when ypu press start processing)? They sometimes
> silently crash (just disappear) which makes the processing slow down or
> even stop when they are all gone.
>
> Best,
> Chris
>
> 216em...@gmail.com schrieb am Freitag, 22. Juli 2022 um 23:17:36 UTC+2:
>
>> Hi all,
>>
>> Our lab is trying to process a dataset of 10,500 .tiff files. However
>> none of the data move past CTF and motion correction and the processing
>> speed slows down to 24 files/hour. Has anyone else seen this issue before
>> that could provide some guidance? Thanks in advance -
>>
>> Emma
>>
> --

> .
>
